title: Cursed Team-Building
theme: Corporate bonding speedruns a preventable HR meeting.

modifiers.label: Executive Cover
modifiers.description: Leadership has accidentally endorsed the nonsense, which makes everything more dangerous.

modifiers.label: Goose Authority
modifiers.description: The live goose has become a real stakeholder in the decision process.

modifiers.label: Process Brain
modifiers.description: The team keeps answering chaos with committees, decks, and forms.

modifiers.label: Morale Spike
modifiers.description: The room has mistaken one tiny success for a scalable culture strategy.

nodes.prompt: Your manager says today's trust exercise will involve a fog machine and one live goose.
nodes.resultText: {{spotlight}} gets shoved into the center of the offsite and the room chooses how publicly it will fail on them.

socialPrompt.prompt: Who in this room would make the offsite much worse?
socialPrompt.voteIntro: The room picked {{spotlight}}. Decide how to make this trust exercise their problem.
socialPrompt.receiptTemplate: {{count}} players said {{spotlight}} would turn this offsite into an HR incident.

choices.label: Make {{spotlight}} follow the goose
choices.resultText: {{spotlight}} decides the goose has the strongest leadership energy in the building.

choices.label: Make {{spotlight}} pitch a synergy deck
choices.resultText: {{spotlight}} volunteers a slide deck and makes the offsite worse on purpose.

choices.label: Make {{spotlight}} hide in catering
choices.resultText: {{spotlight}} drags morale into a buffet strategy and calls it alignment.

nodes.prompt: The goose leads {{spotlight}} and everyone else into the CEO's meditation pod.
nodes.resultText: The room converts a bird incident into executive disruption with {{spotlight}} at the center of it.

choices.label: Have {{spotlight}} call it innovation
choices.resultText: {{spotlight}} reframes a bird incident as disruptive thinking and drags leadership into it.

choices.label: Make {{spotlight}} elect the goose interim chair
choices.resultText: {{spotlight}} lets democracy fail instantly and the goose acquires executive authority.

nodes.prompt: {{spotlight}} opens the slide deck with a typo so bad that finance starts crying.
nodes.resultText: The room decides whether {{spotlight}} should double down or build process around the mistake.

roundTemplate.privatePrompt: Who in this room could still sell this deck to leadership?
roundTemplate.voteIntro: The room thinks {{spotlight}} can still spin this. Decide how they try.
roundTemplate.receiptTemplate: {{count}} players privately said {{spotlight}} could carry the typo deck.

choices.label: Make {{spotlight}} present harder
choices.resultText: {{spotlight}} mistakes volume for recovery and turns the typo into a company-wide event.

choices.label: Make {{spotlight}} create a correction task force
choices.resultText: {{spotlight}} responds to one typo with a full governance model.

nodes.prompt: {{spotlight}} discovers that catering is only sparkling water and one suspicious sheet cake.
nodes.resultText: The room chooses the exact flavor of morale collapse to pin on {{spotlight}}.

choices.label: Tell {{spotlight}} to launch a cake committee
choices.resultText: {{spotlight}} creates governance around one suspicious cake and accidentally triggers HR.

choices.label: Tell {{spotlight}} to toast to surviving this
choices.resultText: {{spotlight}} delivers a tiny morale bump that convinces everyone they can absolutely make this worse.

nodes.prompt: An executive assistant walks in, sees the goose in the pod, and assumes this is a new leadership ritual.

promptVariants.text: An executive assistant walks in, sees the goose in the pod, and congratulates the team on pioneering 'animal-guided leadership.'

nodes.resultText: The room must decide whether to protect the illusion or sell it harder.

choices.label: Turn it into a keynote
choices.resultText: The room builds strategy slides around a bird because a title makes anything sound deliberate.

choices.label: Send the goose on a listening tour
choices.resultText: The room decentralizes authority directly into feathers.

nodes.prompt: The goose snaps at a vice president and morale skyrockets for reasons nobody can defend.
nodes.resultText: The room weaponizes the bird or tries to operationalize it.

roundTemplate.privatePrompt: Who in this room would absolutely overcommit to a goose-led org chart?
roundTemplate.voteIntro: The room thinks {{spotlight}} can sell the goose strategy. Decide how they try.
roundTemplate.receiptTemplate: {{count}} players privately said {{spotlight}} could escalate the goose problem best.

choices.label: Put the goose in charge of feedback
choices.resultText: The room outsources truth-telling to wildlife.

choices.label: Draft a goose escalation ladder
choices.resultText: The room invents process for situations it should have prevented.

nodes.prompt: The typo deck somehow inspires applause from sales, who think this is a bold anti-perfectionism stance.
nodes.resultText: The room chooses whether to bottle the chaos or let sales rename it.

choices.label: Brand it as raw authenticity
choices.resultText: The room upgrades an error into a campaign.

choices.label: Schedule an apology summit
choices.resultText: The room responds to applause with a full internal incident plan.

nodes.prompt: The correction task force already has a Slack channel, a charter, and one internal enemy.
nodes.resultText: The room can either formalize the nonsense or lose control of it.

roundTemplate.privatePrompt: Privately, how would you actually respond to this process mess?
roundTemplate.voteIntro: Privately, the room leaned {{option}}. Now decide what the team officially commits to.
roundTemplate.receiptTemplate: {{count}} players privately picked {{option}} before the team made it public.

choices.label: Expand it into a steering committee
choices.resultText: The room discovers that process can absolutely become a live hazard.

choices.label: Invite leadership to observe
choices.resultText: The team escalates the problem by making it visible upward.

nodes.prompt: The cake committee now has bylaws and a split caucus on frosting transparency.
nodes.resultText: The room chooses whether to protect morale or weaponize governance.

choices.label: Hold a tasting referendum
choices.resultText: The room invites democracy into a situation that did not need it.

choices.label: Appoint a cake czar
choices.resultText: The room solves committee fatigue with monarchy.

nodes.prompt: One sincere toast makes everyone briefly believe this offsite is salvageable.

promptVariants.text: One sincere toast makes everyone briefly believe this offsite is the start of a culture turnaround, which is deeply dangerous.

nodes.resultText: The room now has optimism to misuse.

roundTemplate.privatePrompt: Choose the hidden agenda you want to push before leadership notices.
roundTemplate.voteIntro: Hidden agendas are locked. Decide what the team officially does with this optimism.
roundTemplate.receiptTemplate: {{count}} players secretly pushed {{option}} before the team made it public.
roundTemplate.distributionIntro: {{count}} private reads clustered around {{option}} before the team had to choose out loud.

choices.label: Open a vulnerable sharing circle
choices.resultText: The room creates a feelings arena directly beside a live goose.

choices.label: Pitch a permanent offsite ritual
choices.resultText: The room tries to institutionalize the worst day of the quarter.

nodes.prompt: Leadership asks for a closing statement before anyone is allowed to leave the offsite.
nodes.resultText: The room can still end this badly, or historically badly.

roundTemplate.privatePrompt: Who in this room should eat the blame if leadership makes this official?
roundTemplate.voteIntro: The room picked {{spotlight}}. Decide how hard to lock them into the closing statement.
roundTemplate.receiptTemplate: {{count}} players quietly pushed {{spotlight}} toward the executive fallout.

choices.label: Declare the goose a leadership framework
choices.resultText: The room translates animal chaos into executive language and nobody stops it.

choices.label: Promise measurable trust outcomes
choices.resultText: You put metrics on vibes and the legal department begins to stir.

nodes.prompt: A regional VP hears about your offsite and asks whether this can roll out company-wide.
nodes.resultText: The room has to decide whether to protect the org or doom it at scale.

choices.label: Offer a pilot program
choices.resultText: The room converts one bad day into a replicable playbook.

choices.label: Claim it only works with the goose
choices.resultText: The room ties scalability to a single hostile bird.

nodes.prompt: Operations discovers your side process and wants to know who approved any of this.

promptVariants.text: Operations discovers your side process, but leadership has already begun calling it a courageous experiment.

nodes.resultText: The room chooses between punishment, paperwork, and absurd success.

roundTemplate.privatePrompt: Privately, what would you actually do once operations comes looking?
roundTemplate.voteIntro: Privately, the room leaned {{option}}. Now decide what the team publicly commits to.
roundTemplate.receiptTemplate: {{count}} players privately picked {{option}} before the team answered operations.
roundTemplate.distributionIntro: {{count}} private reads clustered around {{option}} before operations got a response.

choices.label: Bury them in documentation
choices.resultText: The room answers scrutiny with more forms than anyone can absorb.

choices.label: Invite ops into the chaos
choices.resultText: The room widens the blast radius and calls it alignment.

nodes.prompt: HR asks for the official narrative before rumors fill in the blanks.
nodes.resultText: The room now has one last chance to frame the disaster.

choices.label: Call it restorative trust theater
choices.resultText: The room invents a phrase so confident that HR has to write it down.

choices.label: Blame the fog machine vendor
choices.resultText: Accountability evaporates into smoke and procurement gets dragged into it.

nodes.prompt: The room deadlocks so badly that the goose slaps a laminated values poster off the wall and everyone interprets it as a sign.
nodes.resultText: Audience intervention arrives via wildlife and suddenly the room has permission to escalate.

choices.label: Treat it as a mandate for pilot rollout
choices.resultText: The room mistakes symbolism for executive approval.

choices.label: Spin it into a leadership framework
choices.resultText: The room uses a bird tantrum as strategic evidence.

nodes.prompt: A keynote coach in the next conference room hears the commotion and volunteers to help you 'land the lesson.'
nodes.resultText: The room acquires an expensive stranger with dangerous levels of confidence.

choices.label: Use the coach to polish the lie
choices.resultText: The room upgrades nonsense into executive diction.

choices.label: Send the coach to the pilot pitch
choices.resultText: The room outsources credibility and keeps all the risk.

nodes.prompt: A neighboring workshop mistakes your cake referendum for a live innovation lab and starts taking notes.
nodes.resultText: The room gains external observers who are far too enthusiastic.

choices.label: Absorb them into the committee
choices.resultText: The room scales dysfunction by inviting fresh witnesses.

choices.label: Let them publish the findings
choices.resultText: The room outsources narrative control in exchange for buzz.

nodes.prompt: The goose is promoted ahead of the entire product org and somehow posts immediate trust gains.
HR describes the offsite as unforgettable for all the wrong legal reasons and schedules six follow-up trainings.
Corporate rolls your accidental ritual into three regions before anyone can admit it was a mistake.
The team leaves with morale at an all-time high and zero shared understanding of what actually happened.
Procurement blames the fog machine, finance blames the deck, and the goose remains the only respected leader in the building.
